page: Amazon Web Services m6g.medium is a general-purpose server powered by a single-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 1 dedicated physical core, 1 thread, and a CPU clock speed of 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the server is positioned for entry-level workloads. In stress-ng single-core testing, the CPU achieved an average score of 1499.79, though its single-core limitation results in a matching multi-core score of 1499.79, which falls into the lower performance tier. Memory bandwidth tests show 59.31 GB/sec for cached small blocks, decreasing to 19.48 GB/sec for read operations and 9.61 GB/sec for write operations on large uncached blocks. Other benchmarks, such as Geekbench (923.0 single-core and multi-core) and Passmark (658.76 CPU score), reflect the resource constraints of a single-core ARMv8 configuration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6g.medium offers a low-cost entry point into the AWS Graviton ecosystem, providing dedicated CPU allocation without the credit-depletion risks of burstable instances. However, the hardware profile presents clear tradeoffs, particularly in multi-threaded tasks, database operations (628.47 Passmark database score), and static web serving (1.14 GB/sec throughput). This server is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, single-threaded application testing, and small-scale Redis deployments.
description: Amazon Web Services m6g.medium is a general-purpose instance featuring a single-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It provides 1 dedicated vCPU, 4.0 GB of memory, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor without local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, but the single-core architecture limits multi-threaded capabilities, resulting in lower scores across Geekbench, Passmark, and memory bandwidth evaluations. Qualitatively, this server offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for ARM64 development and lightweight workloads, avoiding the CPU credit limitations of burstable instances. It is best suited for basic web serving, single-threaded applications, and lightweight background tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.medium is a general-purpose arm64 server featuring 1 dedicated Graviton vCPU, 4.0 GB RAM, and Nitro virtualization, designed for lightweight workloads and ARM development.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.medium is a general-purpose arm64 server with 1 Graviton vCPU, 4.0 GB RAM, and average single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A dedicated single-core ARM64 instance providing a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio for lightweight workloads and entry-level development.

4.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Dedicated CPU allocation on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with no local storage
Average single-core performance in stress-ng CPU benchmarks
Best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded tasks, and ARM64 testing
